_note: Drill questions matched per function. Each question is engineered to reliably ELICIT its function's language with minimal decision-load — the student parrots the function's phrases against prompt after prompt of the same shape until the frame becomes automatic. Keyed by function id (matching functional-english.json). B2/C1 Part-4 style: view/intellectual prompts on familiar topics.

advantages: What are the advantages of living in a big city?
What are the benefits of learning a foreign language?
What are the advantages of studying online rather than in a classroom?
What are the good points about working from home?
What are the advantages of using public transport?
What are the benefits of doing regular exercise?
disadvantages: What are the disadvantages of spending a lot of time on social media?
What are the drawbacks of living in a big city?
What are the downsides of studying abroad?
What are the disadvantages of relying on technology?
What are the drawbacks of working from home?
What are the disadvantages of fast food becoming so popular?
problems: What problems does heavy traffic cause in cities?
What problems does social media create for young people?
What problems does tourism bring to popular destinations?
What are the main problems facing students today?
What problems does pollution cause in modern cities?
What problems can arise from working very long hours?
solution: How could cities reduce traffic and pollution?
What could be done to help young people use social media more safely?
How could schools make learning more enjoyable?
What can be done to protect the environment in your area?
How could people be encouraged to eat more healthily?
What could be done to reduce stress among students?
cause: Why do so many young people move to cities?
Why is it difficult to learn a foreign language?
Why are people spending more time online these days?
Why do you think obesity is increasing in many countries?
Why do some students find exams so stressful?
Why has fast food become so popular around the world?
effect: How does living in a city affect people's daily lives?
How does social media affect the way young people communicate?
How does working from home affect people's routines?
What effect does pollution have on people's health?
How does learning a language change the way you see the world?
How does tourism affect small local communities?
justifying: Do you think students should study subjects they enjoy? Why?
Should young people be encouraged to travel? Give your reasons.
Do you think everyone should learn a second language? Why?
Should schools teach more practical life skills? Why?
Do you think cities are better places to live than the countryside? Give reasons.
Should people spend less time on their phones? Why?
opinion: What do you think about people spending so much time on social media?
What is your view on studying abroad?
What do you think about the way technology is changing our lives?
What is your opinion on working from home?
What do you think about the importance of learning English today?
What is your view on the amount of homework students are given?
agree: Some people say technology has made our lives easier. Do you agree?
'Travel is the best form of education.' Do you agree?
Some say cities offer a better quality of life. Do you agree?
'Learning a language opens more doors than any other skill.' Do you agree?
Some people believe exams are the fairest way to test students. Do you agree?
'Social media brings people closer together.' Do you agree?
disagree: Some people say social media makes us less sociable. What would you say against that?
'Studying abroad is always better than studying at home.' Do you disagree?
Some claim that technology has made our lives less meaningful. What would you argue?
'City life is more stressful than it is worth.' Do you disagree?
Some say young people today are lazier than in the past. What would you say?
'Exams are the only real measure of ability.' Do you disagree?
partial: 'Living in a city is better than living in the countryside.' How far do you agree?
'Technology has done more good than harm.' To what extent do you agree?
'Studying abroad is worth the cost.' How far do you agree?
'Social media is bad for young people.' To what extent do you agree?
'It is better to specialise early than to study many subjects.' How far do you agree?
'People should always follow their passion when choosing a career.' To what extent do you agree?
speculation: How will life be different in fifty years' time?
How do you think we will travel in the future?
How might schools change in the coming years?
What do you think cities will be like in the future?
How will technology change the way we work?
How do you think communication will develop in the future?
generalising: In general, do people prefer city life or country life?
On the whole, how do young people in your country spend their free time?
Broadly speaking, how important is technology in people's daily lives?
In most cases, why do people decide to learn English?
Generally, how healthy are people's lifestyles these days?
On the whole, how do most students prepare for their exams?
comparing: Which do you prefer, watching TV or using social media?
How does living in a city compare with living in the countryside?
Which is better, studying at home or studying abroad?
How does travelling by train compare with travelling by car?
Which do you prefer, reading books or watching films?
How does online learning compare with learning in a classroom?
possibility: What might happen if we all switched to electric cars?
What could life be like if everyone worked from home?
What might change if English stopped being the world's main language?
What could happen if cities kept growing at this rate?
What might the effects be if schools had no exams?
What could happen if people spent less time online?
probability: How likely is it that most people will work from home in the future?
How likely are we to solve the problem of pollution in cities?
How likely is it that electric cars will replace petrol cars?
How likely is it that young people will keep moving to cities?
How likely is it that social media will become even more popular?
How likely is it that we will all speak English one day?
similarities: What do city life and country life have in common?
In what ways are online learning and classroom learning alike?
What do reading and travelling have in common as ways of learning?
In what ways are TV and social media similar?
What do all successful students seem to have in common?
In what ways are studying abroad and studying at home alike?
differences: What are the main differences between city life and country life?
How is life today different from life fifty years ago?
What are the differences between studying online and in a classroom?
How is travelling by train different from travelling by plane?
What are the differences between how young and old people use technology?
How is learning a language at school different from learning it abroad?
trends: What are the most obvious changes you can see in the way people communicate?
How have people's shopping habits changed in recent years?
What changes have you noticed in the way young people spend their time?
How has technology changed the way we study?
What are the biggest changes you can see in your home town?
How have people's eating habits changed over the years?
quantities: How many young people do you think use social media every day?
How much time do most students spend studying each week?
How many people in your country prefer city life to country life?
How much do people rely on their phones these days?
How many people do you think will own an electric car in ten years?
How much of your day do you spend using technology?
exceptions: Is there any situation where living in a city is not the better choice?
Are there any cases where technology does not make life easier?
Is there ever a time when studying abroad is not worth it?
Are there any exceptions to the idea that exercise is good for you?
Is there any situation where social media is genuinely useful for young people?
Are there any cases where working from home simply does not work?
